explanation:
The vulnerability is valid. The _calcInvestorRefRewards function (line 689-697) uses the CURRENT isInvestorInPool[investor][2] status to calculate referral rewards for ALL rounds since the last updateRefRewardTimestamp, retroactively applying the downline bonus (downlineRefsDeposit * 675 / BASIS_POINTS) to rounds when the investor was NOT in pool 2.

The root cause is that deposit() does NOT call _updateInvestorRefReward() for the depositor (only for the referer chain via _updateReferers at line 600), yet it CAN add the depositor to pool 2 via _checkAndAddInvestorToPool in _updatePoolRewards (line 601). This creates an unsettled gap: the investor joins pool 2 without their pending referral rewards being calculated first.

Exploitable scenario: An investor with sufficient directRefsCount and directRefsDeposit but insufficient totalDeposit waits many rounds, then deposits to increase totalDeposit, then deposits again (needed because totalDeposit is updated at line 609, AFTER _updatePoolRewards at line 601). The second deposit triggers pool 2 addition with the updated totalDeposit, while updateRefRewardTimestamp remains stale. On claimReward(), the downline bonus is applied retroactively for all elapsed rounds.

Note: The finding's specific exploit scenario has a minor inaccuracy (claiming a single deposit suffices to both cross the totalDeposit threshold and trigger pool addition), but the core logic flaw is real and exploitable with an extra deposit step.
